name: Portabella Mushroom Mozzarella
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil -- or less
1 cup chunky tomato sauce with onion, celery, green bell pepper
3 ounces mozzarella cheese, part skim milk (healthy choice)
recipeInstructions:
The idea is to substitute portabella mushroom for the meat in a favorite Italian recipe. This serves 2 easily and would have served 3.
Pasta Zesta® is a new product for us. It's a spaghetti made of durum wheat, flavored with various herbs (we chose tomato and basil), no eggs! Very light and flavorful. Cooks in 3 minutes. Comes in a tan 8-oz box near the bottled sauces. Made in Canada.
STEP #1 Using a microwave-safe dish, combine the tomato sauce, garlic, basil, oregano, and red pepper flakes. Microwave at 100% for about 2 minutes (700 watts). Stir and let stand.
MEANWHILE prepare the pasta.
STEP #2 Heat oil in a skillet. Press some toasted wheat germ into the mushroom slices and sauté over medium-high heat, turning once, about 1 1/2 to 2 minutes per side. Remove from heat.
STEP #3 Set oven temperature to 375°F. Place drained pasta in a shallow casserole dish. Pour half the sauce on top and spread. Season with pepper (a little salt). Sprinkle remaining wheat germ along the outer edge where it will catch the sauce. Layer the mushroom slices on top of that. Pour on the remaining sauce. Top with cheese. Top with parsley. Bake about 15 minutes until cheese is melted and beginning to brown. Nice served with asparagus.
